notes: Well, I tested this game 3 times. On the first try game just crashed after I press "Play" - turns out that my video driver was broken somehow. On the second try with reinstalled 396 driver I was able to play the game, but FPS was dropping to 30 very frequently (Even with mid-low settings). On the last try with new 415 driver I was able to play the game with Max settings on 60 fps - real drops was only when I ignite the bonefire.

notes: Runs out of the box but has some startup/shutdown/performance issues. After running for some time, when exiting the game the desktop freezes: no mouse movement, no access to virtual console etc.  Also while performance for dark souls 2 was phenomenal, dark souls 3 runs worse on much lower settings.
title: DARK SOULS™ III

notes: There were some rare stuttering but otherwise it's been silky smooth at 4K even when GNOME 3's built-in blue light limiter kicked in at the end of my session. I was moving it to another desktop, tabbing out and in and overall not being very careful and it didn't crash; you could have fooled me into thinking this was a native port. I only got to the first Evangelist in Undead Settlement before quitting for today.

title: DARK SOULS™ III

notes:
Game worked perfect at first for about a week. Then it would not run at all. My logs showed this error: Cannot open assembly '~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/DARK SOULS III/Game/DarkSoulsIII.exe 
Right clicking on the game and selecting properties, then checking "Force the use of a specific Steam Play compatibility tool", then selecting "Proton 3.16-6 Beta" fixed everything for me. Game now runs perfect once again.
title: DARK SOULS™ III

notes: Works perfectly. Make sure to use Proton 3.16-6 or newer, as earlier versions has some minor issues.

title: DARK SOULS™ III

notes: The game runs, but the performance is shit. Some animations seem to drop the framerate to about 20-25 fps, when those animations are over you get stable 60 fps again. The framedrops accrue most of the time while fighting. Changing the resolution or graphics quality does not affect the framerate at all. Changing the resolution seems to fix a occurring framedrop, but soon after a new framedrop will kick in. Now pleasurable to play, but I will continue to play that way in coop, cause windows is even a worse user experience. Game overall is very stable, just framerate issues.

notes: The game will not start new game if you'r sound configuration are more than 2.1. Also cut-scenes will not play too(i was wondering why game cannot start with 5.1 sound after select New Game, create character and after done it, game will not start). So make sure you have 2.1 or just stereo sound selected, before start the game. In some areas with more fog and dust(like fight with Dancer and the Dreg Heap location) i have massive FPS loss, if look in specified direction. But even with 2.1 sound, after some hours of play, sound will get distorted and change frequency. Only game restart fix it.

notes:
Runs perfectly fine, I was getting around 45fps on full HD medium settings. Which is almost the same performance I was getting on windows (only 6~10 less fps).


Just a FYI if if you can't run it and you have the game installed on a second hard drive. Make sure the second hard drive is not ntfs. I wasn't able to run the game until I installed on my main ext4 ssd. Also update your drivers, the 390 nvidia drivers that ubuntu recommends are severely out of date and will cause huge frame drops during the game.

title: DARK SOULS™ III

notes:
Optimus graphics with Proton produce ugly hangs (2-3 seconds if not unrecoverable) unless VSYNC is disabled.  You cannot disable VSYNC in the game's settings or with command line parameters, so ESYNC must be disabled to avoid the ugly hangs.  The ugly hangs are replaced by stuttering, but it is at least playable.

I would like to see a feature in newer Proton versions to force off VSYNC in Vulkan for Optimus systems.

title: DARK SOULS™ III

notes: It works out of the box on 60fps, even with Max graphics settings at 1440p. All the tweaks suggested in forums and reports have been pointless. In my case, the system kernel and processes can introduce some issues in the game performance: ordinary Linux distros such as Ubuntu and Manjaro cannot perform well on my CPU. The only distros that have worked and provided the desired performance are Debian and SteamOS.
